title: Operating System Internals
summary: Covers all major OS subsystems: kernel/user mode separation, system calls, device drivers, process management (states, multitasking, threads), memory management (segmentation with descriptors and privilege levels, pagination with page tables and TLB, virtual memory with demand paging), inter-process communication (shared memory, semaphores), MMU usage, and program creation (compilation, linking, relocation, shared libraries).
learning_objectives: Explain the role and structure of the operating system
Describe system calls and device drivers
Perform address translation using segmentation and pagination
Explain virtual memory and demand paging
Trace program creation from source to execution
